title: Impatiens minor
intro: Impatiens minor, also known as Small Balsam or Pale Touch-me-not, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Balsam family. This plant is traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Impatiens minor include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Impatiens minor is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Impatiens minor is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Balsamaceae. It is a small annual herb native to Southeast Asia. Impatiens minor is placed in the genus Impatiens, which includes various species of balsam plants. The plant's classification is further detailed as Impatiens minor (Burm.f.) DC. This classification places it within the order Ericales, a group of flowering plants.
